title: x-tra spicy deep fried moroccan lamb briouat
description: deep-fried, umami-packed, moroccan lamb briouat with a crispy exterior and a glassy-jelly center, all wrapped in a delicate pastry and topped with parmesan cheese.
instructions: in a blender, combine 1/2 cup of lamb broth, 1/4 cup of olive oil, 2 cloves of garlic, 1 tablespoon of ground cumin, 1 teaspoon of ground coriander, 1/2 teaspoon of ground cinnamon, 1/4 teaspoon of ground cayenne pepper, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
blend the mixture until smooth and set aside.
in a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat.
add 1 pound of lamb shoulder, cut into small pieces, and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
add 1 onion, diced, and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for 1 minute.
add the blended mixture and cook for 2 minutes.
stir in 1 cup of all-purpose flour and cook for 1 minute.
stir in 1/2 cup of grated parmesan cheese and cook until melted.
cover the skillet and cook for 5 minutes.
uncover the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
set the filling aside to cool.
on a lightly floured surface, roll out 1 package of puff pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
cut the pastry into 8 equal squares.
place 1/4 cup of the lamb filling in the center of each square.
brush the edges of the pastry with 1 egg, beaten, and fold the pastry over the filling to form a triangle.
press the edges to seal the briouat and place on a baking sheet.
deep-fry the briouat in 2-3 inches of vegetable oil until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
drain the briouat on paper towels and serve hot.
note: for an extra crispy exterior, you can chill the briouat in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before frying.
